author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
The Pelagian Grace of ‘Capacity’ Exploded. The Scripture Teaches the Need of God’s Help in Doing, Speaking, and Thinking, Alike.
title: The Pelagian Grace of ‘Capacity’ Exploded. The Scripture Teaches the Need of God’s Help in Doing, Speaking, and Thinking, Alike.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 26 [XXV.]—The Pelagian Grace of “Capacity” Exploded.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The Scripture Teaches the Need of God’s Help in Doing, Speaking, and Thinking, Alike.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Let Pelagius, therefore, cease at last to deceive both himself and others by his disputations against the grace of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It is not on account of only one of these three—that is to say, of the “capacity” of a good will and work—that the grace of God towards us ought to be proclaimed; but also on account of the good “will” and “work” themselves.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: This “capacity,” indeed, according to his definition, avails for both directions; and yet our sins must not also be attributed to God in consequence, as our good actions, according to his view, are attributed to Him owing to the same capacity.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It is not only, therefore, on this account that the help of God’s grace is maintained, because it assists our natural capacity.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He must cease to say, “That we are able to do, say, think any good, is from Him who has given us this ability, and who also assists this ability; whereas that we really do a good thing, or speak a good word, or think a good thought, proceeds from our own selves.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He must, I repeat, cease to say this.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For God has not only given us the ability and aids it, but He further works in us “to will and to do.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It is not because we do not will, or do not do, that we will and do nothing good, but because we are without His help.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: How can he say, “That we are able to do good is of God, but that we actually do it is of ourselves,” when the apostle tells us that he “prays to God” in behalf of those to whom he was writing, “that they should do no evil, but that they should do that which is good?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: His words are not, “We pray that ye be able to do nothing evil;” but, “that ye do no evil.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Neither does he say, “that ye be able to do good;” but, “that ye do good.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Forasmuch as it is written, “As many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God,” it follows that, in order that they may do that which is good, they must be led by Him who is good.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: How can Pelagius say, “That we are able to make a good use of speech comes from God; but that we do actually make this good use of speech proceeds from ourselves,” when the Lord declares, “It is the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He does not say, “It is not you who have given to yourselves the power of speaking well;” but His words are, “It is not ye that speak.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Nor does He say, “It is the Spirit of your Father which giveth, or hath given, you the power to speak well;” but He says, “which speaketh in you.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He does not allude to the motion of “the capacity,” but He asserts the effect of the co-operation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: How can this arrogant asserter of free will say, “That we are able to think a good thought comes from God, but that we actually think a good thought proceeds from ourselves”?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He has his answer from the humble preacher of grace, who says, “Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think anything as of ourselves, but our sufficiency is of God.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Observe he does not say, “to be able to think anything;” but, “to think anything.”
